title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices pulled back 0.81% to $3,591.26 per ton, while Chinese domestic markets traded at ¥24,551 per ton. The minor dip is driven by early signals of restored commercial vessel flows through the Strait of Hormuz, easing geopolitical supply fears. Despite the pullback, critically low LME inventories, elevated energy costs, and damage to Middle Eastern smelting infrastructure provide strong underlying price support.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum fell to 3603 USD/T on April 24, 2026, down 0.46% from the previous day, though up 11.02% over the past month and 47.82% over the year. Aluminum futures in the UK held above $3,600 per tonne near a four-year high due to the continued blockage of the Strait of Hormuz threatening Middle Eastern supply disruptions. Aluminum is expected to trade at 3643.69 USD/Tonne by the end of this quarter.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: The United States faces a severe aluminum supply crisis, with Bloomberg warning that supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum. This tariff, intended to protect domestic production, has triggered disruptions threatening the manufacturing base. Middle East production issues have eliminated major foreign sources, and Bank of America warns of a potential 1.5 million ton deficit.

title: Aluminum Supply Shock Raises Risks For Global Metals Markets
summary: An aluminum supply shock from Middle East disruptions, including reduced operations in Qatar (60% capacity) and curtailments in Iran and Iraq smelters, threatens up to 50% of regional supply. This equals 11% of non-China production and 5% of global output, with potential long-term impacts from smelter damage. Global production targets are adjusted downward amid these risks.

title: It's not just oil — the Iran war is disrupting helium and aluminum
summary: The Iran war disrupts global helium and aluminum supplies, critical for semiconductors, medical equipment, and packaging. Gulf countries produce 9% of world aluminum, and local disruptions are tightening supply while raising energy costs. Aluminum prices hit a four-year high, potentially increasing U.S. consumer costs for food and goods.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3603 USD/T on April 24, 2026, but remain up 11.02% over the past month and 47.82% yearly. UK aluminum futures hold above $3,600 per tonne near a four-year high due to Strait of Hormuz blockage threatening prolonged Middle East supply disruptions.

title: Aluminium production hit by the Middle East conflict: Nearly 4% in Q1 and 32
summary: Middle East conflict has hit aluminum production by nearly 4% in Q1, with rising geopolitical tensions causing early production disruptions and tighter supply. China's alumina production grew 8% in Q3 2025 to 22.7 million tonnes, outpacing primary aluminum and signaling surplus pressure.

title: Aluminum industries latest news
summary: Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are impacting the global aluminum market with production disruptions and tighter supply conditions.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,105 USD/T on April 24, 2026, down 0.36% from the previous day. Recently, it increased to 1062.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024, with a 9.15% gain over the past 4 weeks.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel rose to 3,152 CNY/T on April 24, 2026, up 0.54% from the previous day. Steel rebar futures traded at around CNY 3,130 per ton, near a three-week high due to firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices for next month by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
summary: Italian HRC index rose by 10% from end-December to €694.25/t ex-works on April 22. Indian HRC offers were heard at $705–710/t cfr EU for May. US steelmaker Steel Dynamics reported stable ferrous scrap flows, with average external finished steel sales prices at $1,193/short ton in Q1.

title: Steel Market Update: Home
summary: The price gap between US hot-rolled coil and landed offshore product tightened this week as both domestic and import prices trended higher. Sheet market sources report strengthening market conditions with steady to elevated demand and spot prices edging up.

title: World crude steel production for March 2026
source: Steel Market Update / World Steel Association (worldsteel)
summary: World crude steel production totaled 159.9 million metric tons in March 2026 across 69 reporting countries. Production jumped 12.8% from February but declined 4.0% compared to March 2025.

title: US domestic raw steel production week ending April 18, 2026
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,848,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 80.0%. Production was distributed across regions: Great Lakes (525k tons), Southern (823k tons), Midwest (295k tons), North East (133k tons), and Western (72k tons).

title: US steel production surges, poised to replace imports
summary: US steel production is up 8.2% year-over-year with capacity utilization at 79.1%, positioning the industry to replace imports.

title: China's crude steel production up again in mid-April
summary: China's steel production continued to increase at a slower pace in mid-April 2026.

title: Stegra Receives €1.4 Billion in New Financing Round for Green Steel Plant
summary: Stegra announced a €1.4 billion financing round led by a Wallenberg Investments-led consortium to support its green steel plant in Boden, Sweden. This investment represents a significant commitment to advancing sustainable steel production technology.

title: FIMIGroup to Deliver Heavy-Gauge Leveling and Cut-to-Length Lines to JSW Steel
summary: FIMIGroup announced plans to deliver two new heavy-gauge leveling and cut-to-length lines to JSW Steel in India. This equipment delivery represents technological advancement in steel processing capabilities.

title: Air Liquide Invests Over $350 Million in Louisiana for Low-Carbon Steel Production
summary: Air Liquide announced a major investment exceeding $350 million to strengthen its operations in Louisiana while supporting the growing demand for low-carbon steel production. This investment reflects industry momentum toward sustainable steelmaking technologies.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent disruptions in production.

title: Scientists Develop Dirt-powered Fuel Cell That Could Replace Batteries
summary: Scientists have developed a fuel cell that uses microbes in soil to produce electricity. The device can power underground sensors for tasks like monitoring moisture or detecting touch, without needing batteries or solar panels, and works in both dry and wet conditions.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures traded above $18,750, hovering close to a more than three-month high of $18,775, with the market consolidating after a strong rally. Nickel rose to 19,125 USD/T on April 24, 2026, up 1.86% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has risen 10.26%, and is up 23.47% compared to the same time last year.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices rallied over 2% to $19,098.29 per ton, driven by supply-side tightening and policy shifts. The International Nickel Study Group's projection of an impending market deficit has shifted sentiment. Indonesia's revised nickel ore benchmark pricing, elevated HPAL input costs, and Middle East tensions are constraining supply.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: The current price of nickel futures is 19,113.75, with a previous close of 18,690.75. Nickel prices outlook remains bearish as UBS warns of persistent surplus through 2026 despite production cuts.
